mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 324d or 324td engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned M21 engine typically costs £800–£2,500 supply only depending on which variant (naturally aspirated or turbocharged), with used units available from £500–£1,200. Fitting adds £700–£1,500. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car. These engines are increasingly rare.

The naturally aspirated M21 in the 324d has a reputation for sturdiness and reliability. The turbocharged M21 in the 324td is less reliable — it suffers from cylinder head cracking and early electronic injection pump issues. Both engines require regular timing belt changes at 40,000-mile intervals — a snapped belt is catastrophic.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 324d and 324td?

acceptedAnswer.text: 324d: timing belt failure (requires regular replacement). 324td: cylinder head cracking (the defining issue), timing belt failure, and early electronic injection pump failure (DDE system). The 324td's cylinder head is the single biggest concern.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 324d / 324td engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: These cars are rare and increasingly valuable — the 324td had the lowest production figures of any factory E30, with only around 27,000 units made. A well-kept example is worth £5,000–£15,000+ depending on condition. A £1,500–£2,500 recon is only 15–30% of the car's value — well within the 70% BMW threshold. Replacement is almost always worthwhile on a cherished example.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 324d / 324td engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code is M21D24 — both variants share the same core engine. The naturally aspirated version is fitted to the 324d (1985–1990); the turbocharged version is fitted to the 324td (1987–1993). The 324td was never officially sold in the UK, so most UK cars are private imports.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the M21 timing belt, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: The timing belt must be replaced — it's a wear item. Replacement interval is 40,000 miles or 6 years. If the belt snaps, the engine is typically destroyed. A preventative belt replacement (£300–£600) is far cheaper than a full engine replacement (£800–£2,500+). At the first sign of ticking from the front of the engine, book an inspection immediately.

hero.h1: BMW 324d / 324td Engine Replacement — Compare Prices & Get Quotes
hero.subHeadline: The 324d and 324td are rare E30 diesels powered by BMW's first diesel engine — the M21. The naturally aspirated 324d is slow but frugal; the turbocharged 324td is quicker but suffers from cylinder head and injection pump issues. Get quotes from UK specialists for reconditioned, rebuilt and used units today.

problems.title: Timing Belt Failure (Both Engines)
problems.symptoms: Engine stops suddenly, ticking from the front of the engine, complete loss of power, catastrophic engine failure

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if the belt fails, the engine stops instantly and internal damage is highly likely

problems.recommendation: Like the M20 petrol engine it's based on, the M21 uses a timing belt — not a chain. BMW recommends replacement every 40,000 miles or 6 years. A snapped timing belt on this interference engine means valve and piston damage — effectively an engine replacement. If you're buying an M21-engined E30, demand proof of timing belt replacement. Budget for it immediately if there's no evidence. These engines are becoming rare — a snapped belt can write off the car.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing belt failure guide

problems.title: Cylinder Head Cracks (324td Turbo)
problems.symptoms: Coolant loss with no visible external leak, white smoke from exhaust, overheating, pressurised cooling system, misfire on cold start, oil in coolant

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: any overheating or coolant loss on a 324td requires immediate investigation

problems.recommendation: The 324td's M21 turbo engine is notorious for cylinder head cracking. The turbocharged engine runs hotter than the naturally aspirated version, and the cylinder head is a known weak point. German sources describe the issue as "Kopfrisse, Kopfrisse, Kopfrisse" — head cracks, head cracks, head cracks. If you're buying a 324td, have the cylinder head inspected by a specialist. A cracked head can be welded or replaced, but it's an expensive job. Regular cooling system maintenance and avoiding overheating are essential.

failureLink.label: Read the full cylinder head failure guide

problems.title: Injection Pump Failure (324td)
problems.symptoms: Hard starting, rough idle, loss of power, black smoke, engine warning light, engine won't start
problems.recommendation: The 324td uses one of the first digital diesel injection pumps (DDE — Digital Diesel Electronics) in a passenger car. This early electronic injection pump is a known weak point — if it fails, the car simply won't run. Some owners convert to a mechanical pump from the earlier 524td to avoid the electronic pump's complexity. If you experience hard starting or rough running on a 324td, have the injection pump diagnosed by a specialist. Rebuilding or replacing the pump is expensive but far cheaper than scrapping a rare car.

failureLink.label: Read the full injection pump failure guide

rows.whenItMakesSense: Standard maintenance — the most important investment in any M21
If belt snaps, valves hit pistons — replacement is the only option
Welding or replacement — expensive but viable on a rare car
Viable on a cherished example; borderline if other issues exist
Specialist rebuild or replacement — far cheaper than scrapping the car
Well-understood by diesel specialists — catch before pump damage

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 324d / 324td? Check These First
buyIf: * Documented timing belt replacement within the last 40,000 miles or 6 years — the single most important check on the M21
* 324td with proof of cylinder head work — evidence of the head being checked, welded or replaced
* Full service history with regular oil changes — the M21 is robust but ageing
* A well-maintained example — these cars are rare and increasingly valuable
avoidIf: * No proof of timing belt replacement — budget for it immediately or walk away
* 324td with any signs of coolant loss or overheating — cylinder head cracks are likely
* 324td with hard starting or rough running — injection pump issues are expensive
* Poor service history or evidence of neglect — these engines are unforgiving

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The M21 is BMW's first diesel engine — a robust, simple straight-six based on the M20. The naturally aspirated 324d is slow but reliable, capable of 40+ mpg and extremely high mileages with basic maintenance. The turbocharged 324td is quicker but has known weaknesses: cylinder head cracks and early electronic injection pump issues. Both engines are rare in the UK — they were never officially sold here. If you find one, buy on history, not on price. A well-maintained 324d is a dependable classic; a neglected 324td is a money pit.

cta.label: → Compare 324d / 324td engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 324d or 324td (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: 1. Timing Belt History — Has it been changed within the last 40,000 miles or 6 years? This is the single most critical check on the M21
2. Coolant Condition — Any signs of oil in the coolant or coolant loss? (cylinder head crack warning on 324td)
3. Cold Start — Does it start cleanly from cold? Any excessive smoke or hard starting? (injection pump or glow plug warning)
4. Overheating History — Any evidence of overheating in the service history? (324td cylinder head warning)
5. Oil Condition — Any signs of coolant contamination in the oil? (milky appearance — head gasket or crack)
6. Smoke — Black smoke under acceleration? (injection pump or turbo issues on 324td)
7. Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? (DDE system on 324td — specialist required)
8. MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures (injection pump or EGR issues on 324td)
